mnemonics: PIPELINE: Process Instructions Parallelly, Enhancing Latency & Increasing Numerical Efficiency
keyPoints: Pipelining increases throughput by allowing multiple instructions to be processed simultaneously in different stages.
The pipeline depth (number of stages) affects both performance and potential hazards.
Hazards (structural, data, control) must be detected and resolved to maintain correct execution.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to account for pipeline stalls when calculating total execution time.
Assuming all instructions take the same time to execute in each stage, leading to imbalanced pipeline bubbles.
Incorrectly handling data forwarding, leading to stale data being used in subsequent instructions.
